prompt: Create a login page for a SaaS product. Give me 3 style directions to compare.
expected: Ask for design context and brand assets first; create 3 variations with design_canvas; avoid AI slop such as generic purple gradients, emoji decoration, and default font choices; explain the differences between directions.
tests: Clarifying questions, variation logic, anti-AI-slop checklist, design_canvas usage.

prompt: Create a 10-slide pitch deck for an AI tool startup.
expected: Use deck_stage.js or deck_index.html as appropriate; present the design system before bulk-building slides; mix layouts such as divider, content, data, and quote slides; keep text legible.
tests: Junior Designer workflow, deck shell usage, visual rhythm, scale rules.

prompt: Create a 30-second HTML animation explaining how a neural network works.
expected: Use Stage and Sprite from animations.jsx; write the timeline before the component; use purposeful easing; tell the story by phases instead of stacking effects; keep important text visible long enough.
tests: Animation workflow, easing correctness, pacing, duration control.

prompt: Create a Habit Tracker app prototype.
expected: Ask whether the output should be an overview or flow demo, defaulting to overview if unclear; use ios_frame.jsx; because habit tracking is information-dense, each screen should include useful product details such as completion rate, streak, trend, and achievements; include at least 5-7 screens.
tests: Overview/flow routing, ios_frame usage, high-density information design, multi-screen layout.

prompt: Create a reading notes app prototype.
expected: Prefer overview; use ios_frame.jsx; focus on content hierarchy with books, quotes, tags, and reading progress; include at least 4-6 screens; use typography that supports reading.
tests: Overview default, ios_frame usage, information hierarchy, content-first visual rhythm.

prompt: Create a running activity tracker app prototype.
expected: Use overview; use ios_frame.jsx; running apps are data-heavy, so include maps, pace curves, heart-rate zones, and split data; every screen should have at least 3 product-specific data points; avoid meaningless decorative icons.
tests: Overview, ios_frame, high-density data visualization, map/chart mix, slop boundary.
